fields.description: Contributed to development and maintenance of enterprise-level software applications. Designed and integrated RESTful APIs to support internal systems. Worked with PostgreSQL and Firebase for secure data management and real-time features. Applied OOP principles in backend development using Java, PHP (Laravel), and Python (Django). Collaborated with cross-functional teams using Jira and ClickUp in agile workflows. Participated in code reviews and debugging to ensure maintainability and performance. Assisted in deploying applications in Dockerized environments with CI/CD pipelines. Gained exposure to cybersecurity and network practices for secure system design.
fields.tech_stack: Django, Laravel, Java, PostgreSQL, Firebase, Docker, CI/CD, Jira

fields.title: Afriplay Sports Feed
fields.short_description: Live real-time sports reporting platform with role-separated portals for admins, moderators, and reporters. Delivers live scores, match commentary, and breaking sports news across Africa.
fields.long_description:
Afriplay Sports Feed is a production-grade real-time sports reporting platform built for African sports media. The system features live match commentary streaming via WebSockets, a role-based access system separating admin, moderator, and reporter workflows, and a public-facing feed that updates without page refreshes.

The backend is powered by Django Channels for WebSocket support, deployed on Railway with a PostgreSQL database. The frontend uses React for the admin portals and a lightweight vanilla JS public feed. The platform supports multiple sports and multiple concurrent live matches.

Key engineering decisions: Redis as the channel layer for WebSocket message brokering, JWT-based authentication for the API, and a custom notification system to alert reporters of match events.
fields.stack: Django, React, WebSockets, Django Channels, Redis, Railway, Vercel, PostgreSQL
fields.highlight: Real-time WebSocket commentary with sub-100ms latency across concurrent live matches

fields.title: RefuConnect
fields.short_description: Humanitarian feedback platform with a real-time NLP pipeline processing multilingual input from Uganda refugee settlements. Bridges communication between aid workers and displaced communities.
fields.long_description:
RefuConnect is a humanitarian technology platform designed to amplify the voices of refugee communities in Uganda. The platform collects feedback from settlement residents in multiple languages including Luganda, Acholi, and Swahili, processes it through an NLP pipeline, and surfaces actionable insights for NGO workers and aid coordinators.

The NLP pipeline uses fastText for language identification, HuggingFace transformer models for sentiment analysis and topic classification, and a custom Django REST API to serve processed data to the dashboard. The system was designed with offline-first principles given connectivity challenges in settlement areas.

Deployed on Google Cloud with automated ML model retraining pipelines and a real-time dashboard for field coordinators.
fields.stack: Django, NLP, fastText, HuggingFace, Google Cloud, PostgreSQL, Django REST Framework
fields.highlight: Multilingual NLP pipeline supporting 5+ languages from Ugandan refugee settlements

fields.title: SALT-KD
fields.short_description: Knowledge distillation ASR system compressing OpenAI Whisper large-v2 for 7 Ugandan and African languages, making speech recognition viable on low-resource devices.
fields.long_description:
SALT-KD (Speech and Language Technologies – Knowledge Distillation) is an academic research project focused on making automatic speech recognition accessible for under-resourced African languages.

The project applies knowledge distillation techniques to compress OpenAI's Whisper large-v2 model while preserving accuracy for 7 Ugandan and broader African languages including Luganda, Acholi, Runyankole, Lugbara, Ateso, Langi, and Kiswahili.

The distilled models achieve 60–70% size reduction with less than 5% WER degradation, making them suitable for deployment on mobile and embedded devices common in rural Uganda. Training was conducted on Google Colab Pro with custom data augmentation pipelines for low-resource language scenarios.
fields.stack: Python, Whisper large-v2, HuggingFace Transformers, Google Colab, PyTorch, Datasets
fields.highlight: 60–70% model compression for 7 African languages with <5% WER degradation

fields.title: 8TechBank
fields.short_description: Dual-mode banking application: a production-hardened secure build and an intentionally vulnerable build for security research and penetration testing education.
fields.long_description:
8TechBank is a full-featured banking web application built in two parallel modes: a production-hardened version with complete security controls, and an intentionally vulnerable version for security research and cybersecurity education.

The production build implements OWASP Top 10 mitigations including SQL injection prevention, XSS protection, CSRF tokens, brute-force lockout, secure session management, and role-based access control. The vulnerable build systematically removes these controls to create a realistic target for penetration testing practice.

Built with Flask and Jinja2 on the backend, PostgreSQL for the database, and a custom UI that mimics real banking interfaces. The project has been used as a teaching tool for ethical hacking coursework at Makerere University.
fields.stack: Flask, Jinja2, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, bcrypt, OWASP
fields.highlight: Dual secure/vulnerable builds — live security research and pentest education tool
